title: Loops, Masonry, Proximity Type, Magnetism & Cmd-K
description: Five live UI drops: a seamless infinite scroll loop, native CSS masonry with a JS fallback, cursor-proximity variable type, magnetic buttons, and a subsequence-match command palette.

drops.title: Seamless Snap-Loop Scroll
drops.description: Fullscreen sections loop forever via CSS scroll-snap and a cloned first section — the reset is never visible.
drops.concept: Instead of computing scroll math, the trick clones the first section and appends it (hidden from a11y) after the last. An IntersectionObserver watches the clone; the instant it crosses ~50% visible, the scroller silently jumps back to real section one. Combined with mandatory scroll-snap and lagging parallax blobs, it reads as an infinite premium-agency loop.
drops.source: Codrops — "The Never Ending Story: Building a Seamless Infinite Scroll Experience with GSAP & Lenis"

interaction.mouse: Scroll to move through sections and trigger the loop seam

drops.title: True Masonry, No JS
drops.description: Native CSS display:masonry behind @supports, with a pixel-identical JS fallback grid for every other browser today.
drops.concept: Chrome shipped an experimental display:masonry behind a flag in 2026, reigniting the years-long CSS Grid Level-3 masonry debate. Since it's not broadly available yet, this drop progressively enhances: a dense CSS Grid with tiny 6px auto-rows and a computed grid-row-end span per card reproduces true masonry packing identically in any browser today.
drops.source: Chrome for Developers — "Brick by brick: Help us build CSS Masonry"

interaction.mouse: Drag sliders to change gap/column width, click Shuffle

drops.title: Cursor-Proximity Variable Weight
drops.description: Every letter's font-weight and scale inflate as the cursor nears it, using a tunable radius and falloff curve.
drops.concept: Designers are moving past whole-word hover states toward per-letter, physically-motivated typography. Each character is tracked individually; distance from the pointer maps through a falloff curve (linear, quadratic, or gaussian) to drive per-letter weight and scale, feeling far more alive than a single hover trigger.

interaction.mouse: Move near any letter to inflate its weight/scale

drops.title: Magnetic Buttons & Cursor Follower
drops.description: Buttons drag toward the cursor within their bounds and spring back on release, with a lagging trail-dot follower.
drops.concept: One of the most-copied micro-interactions in premium agency sites: buttons subtly pull toward the pointer for a tactile, expensive-feeling hover, then overshoot back to rest with a spring ease. A separate lagged cursor-follower dot enlarges over any magnetic target.

interaction.mouse: Hover buttons to feel the pull, move mouse to see the lagging follower
interaction.touch: Custom cursor disabled on touch devices

drops.title: Sloppy-Query ⌘K Palette
drops.description: A command palette that matches by subsequence, not substring — "tgldk" still finds "Toggle dark mode."
drops.concept: A viral "rebuilt in ~60 lines" writeup distilled the Raycast/Linear-style command palette to its essential core: a global shortcut, a subsequence fuzzy matcher, and arrow-key navigation — no framework required. It proves the pattern is tiny enough to own outright instead of importing a heavyweight library.
drops.source: Dev48V (Medium) — "I Rebuilt the Cmd-K Command Palette in ~60 Lines of JavaScript"

interaction.mouse: Click a result to run it
interaction.touch: Tap Open button, tap a result
